channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Coles Wanniassa Supermarket to Remain Open Despite Rumors
item.description: A local community in Wanniassa expressed concern over rumors circulating about the potential closure of the Coles supermarket, sparking a wave of support from residents. The initial post on the Kambah...

item.title: Morocco Advances to World Cup Quarterfinals as France Prepares for Heat Challenge
item.description: Morocco secured their place in the World Cup quarter-finals with a 3-0 victory over Canada, ending the co-hosts' tournament hopes. The Atlas Lions' triumph, marked by goals from Azzedine Ounahi and So...

item.title: Springbok Coach Rassie Erasmus Reflects on Victory Over England Amid Injury Concerns
item.description: Springbok coach Rassie Erasmus expressed satisfaction with the team's 45-21 victory over England at Ellis Park, though he highlighted the significant injury toll. Key players like Ox Nche suffered a s...

item.title: Heat warnings persist in Sudbury as summer temperatures rise
item.description: Greater Sudbury, Ontario, is experiencing an extended heat warning due to persistent hot and humid weather conditions. The situation poses significant challenges for outdoor workers, with temperatures...

item.title: Chhattisgarh Cabinet Focuses on AI, Rural Economy, and Governance Enhancements
item.description: The Chhattisgarh cabinet convened its third 'Chintan Shivir' training session at IIM Raipur, emphasizing artificial intelligence, rural economic development, and modern governance. Chief Minister Vish...

item.title: Morocco Advances to World Cup Quarters with 3-0 Win Against Canada
item.description: Morocco delivered a clinical 3-0 victory over Canada in the FIFA World Cup, marking the first co-host exit from the tournament. The match, held in Houston, saw Morocco's midfielders Azzedine Ounahi an...

item.title: Rediscovered Kodachrome Slides Capture the Vibrant Spirit of the 1957 Rose Parade in Pasadena
item.description: The 1957 Rose Parade in Pasadena, California, marked a vibrant celebration of community, culture, and tradition on New Year’s Day. Rediscovered Kodachrome slides from this event offer a vivid glimpse ...

item.title: Sound Wave Technology Revolutionizes Kidney Stone Treatment in China
item.description: Medical professionals in China are increasingly using sound wave technology to treat kidney stones, offering a non-invasive alternative to traditional surgery. Extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy us...

item.title: All Blacks Edge Out France in High-Stakes Nations Championship Debut
item.description: The All Blacks delivered a hard-fought victory against France in the first-ever Nations Championship Test, showcasing their resilience and tactical discipline. Despite facing challenges, the team's sc...

item.title: 52 Students from Corona School Excel Academically and Extracurricularly
item.description: The Corona School Trust Council celebrated the graduation of 52 students from its Agbara Campus in Ogun State, highlighting their academic excellence and diverse achievements. Principal Innocent Oaikh...

item.title: Alex Eala moves to tears after historic moment against Iga Swiatek at Wimbledon
item.description: Filipino tennis player Alex Eala became emotional after a landmark moment in her career following her match against world-renowned player Iga Swiatek. In an interview after the match, Eala described t...

item.title: 21-Year-Old Arrested for Robbery and Attempted Murder of Woman
item.description: A 21-year-old man, D. Dinesh Reddy, has been arrested by Karimnagar rural police for allegedly pushing a 55-year-old woman labourer into a well after robbing her of her gold mangalsutra and earrings. ...

item.title: Medical Expert Warns Mitch McConnell's Health May Prevent Him from Finishing His Term
item.description: A medical expert has raised serious concerns about the health of U.S. Senator Mitch McConnell (R-KY), suggesting he may be unfit to continue his Senate term. Speech language pathologist Hilary Booco, ...

item.title: External Affairs Minister Jaishankar's Diplomatic Tour to West Asia, US, and Belgium
item.description: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar is embarking on a diplomatic tour that includes visits to four West Asian countries—Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, and Oman—from July 5-10. The trip aims to strengthen ...

item.title: Iran holds mass funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei amid regional conflict
item.description: Tens of thousands of Iranians gathered in Tehran's Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla to pay tribute to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the supreme leader killed during the US-Israeli war on Iran. Mourners, dressed ...

item.title: US Presidents' Influence on NATO Expansion and Its Impact on Russia-Ukraine Relations
item.description: The article discusses how four U.S. presidents contributed to the decline of European NATO by prioritizing a proxy war against Russia, with significant consequences for Ukraine and Europe. UK Prime Mi...

item.title: Joey Chestnut defends hot dog eating contest title amid legal challenges
item.description: The Nathan’s Famous hot dog eating contest is set to resume this Saturday in Coney Island, New York, marking the United States' 250th birthday. Joey Chestnut, the 17-time men's champion with a record ...

item.title: Canadian Player Misses World Cup Knockout Game Due to Hamstring Injury
item.description: Canadian soccer player Jonathan Davies revealed that a lingering hamstring injury prevented him from participating in the World Cup knockout stage. The 31-year-old, who played a key role in Canada's h...

item.title: Call for Musoma Rural to Gain District Status
item.description: Professor Muhongo has urged the Tanzanian government to elevate Musoma Rural to district status, arguing that this administrative change would accelerate development in the region. The proposal comes ...

item.title: Vice Ganda reacts to Kathy and Pol's complex relationship
item.description: The article discusses Vice Ganda's reaction to the 'complicated' love story involving Kathy and Pol, which is a storyline from the TV show 'Everybody Sing.' The piece highlights how Vice Ganda express...
